preamble: The transformation of the voiced alveolar fricative [z] into the voiced velar stop [g] is a form of fortition and hardening, typically documented in the historical development of 'soft' consonants or through complex loanword correspondences.

examples.note: While the shift is usually g > z, the correspondence highlights the historical relationship between these segments in the Romance consonant space.
Historical correspondences where Japanese voiced sibilants map to original velar stops in specific borrowing strata (Go-on/Kan-on).

sources: Phonological research on Sino-Japanese loanword correspondences.
